helpful: Full petition and BIO text in record/documents/ was the single most valuable input — the CFR-after-waiver, amicus count, and the BIO's no-split argument all came straight from the provisioned record.
Per-fee-class Term grant rates in statpack.json (paid 5.4% vs IFP 1.1%) gave a much better anchor than the whole-docket rate.
gaps: No corpus prior matched on citation for student-speech cases; a topic/issue-area facet on `fedcourts query` (e.g. first-amendment/school-speech) would beat citation overlap for SCOTUS cert cells.
A statpack cut for call-for-response (response-requested-after-waiver) petitions would directly price this cell's strongest signal; the relist and CVSG cuts exist but CFR does not.
The prompt references a per-Term 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack table, but the committed statpack.md/json has no such section — either the table or the prompt reference is stale.
notes: Forward cell; SCOTUS e-dockets are not in RECAP so the MCP docket search cannot confirm pendency — the fresh provisioned snapshot covered that instead.
